name: Incorrect Usage of Seeds in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
description: The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) but does not correctly manage seeds. PRNGs are deterministic, so if a seed is reused, predictable, or insufficiently protected, the entire output sequence becomes predictable, allowing attackers to bypass protection mechanisms.
detection_pattern: Look for EXTERNAL[prng_init] or TRANSFORM[seed_generation] nodes where the seed value fed into a TRANSFORM[prng] is not sourced from a cryptographically secure EXTERNAL[csprng|entropy_source] node. The seed may come from a predictable INGRESS[system_time|process_id] or STORAGE[hardcoded_seed], flowing into AUTH[token_generation] or CONTROL[crypto_operation] without a TRANSFORM[secure_seed_derivation] mediator.
